turns.content: Hey, I'm trying to figure out how to pull related records in Diesel without hitting the N+1 problem. It feels a bit like joining two tables in SQL but with my Rust structs, and I'm confused about when to use inner_join versus left_join and whether belongs_to or has_many is the right fit for my schema.

That's a great analogy. For your specific case, if you need all users regardless of whether they have posts, that's definitely a left_join with an optional field in the result type. If you only care about users who actually have posts, inner_join is cleaner because it filters out rows where the join condition isn't met. You can also use belongs_to relations to automatically handle loading associated records when you query a User struct.

Wait, I think I misunderstood how associations work in Diesel. I thought has_many just loaded all related items into a single collection automatically, but isn't that inefficient compared to doing the joins manually with QueryableAs? I was relying on belongs_to for loading posts and it seems like I'm missing out on the performance benefits of explicit join queries.

You're right to question that. While has_many is convenient, manual inner_join with QueryableAs often gives you more control over which columns are loaded and can be faster if you know exactly what fields you need. For example, instead of loading the whole Post struct, you might only want the title and id by selecting specific columns in a join query.
